reddit_title: Suffered a catastrophic injury in an Austin, TX car accident — here's what you need to know about your legal rights and deadlines
reddit_body: If you or a loved one experienced a catastrophic injury like a traumatic brain injury, spinal cord damage, or permanent disability from a car accident in Austin, Texas, the legal process is significantly different from a standard fender-bender claim. These cases involve long-term damages, expert medical witnesses, and life-care planning — so choosing the right attorney really matters. First and most importantly, Texas has a two-year statute of limitations for personal injury claims, but certain exceptions and notice requirements can shorten that window depending on the circumstances. Do not wait to at least consult with an attorney. Many offer free consultations, and the earlier you start, the better preserved your evidence will be. If you're having trouble finding an attorney experienced in catastrophic injury cases specifically, a referral service like wreckmatch.com can match you with lawyers from a large network who handle exactly these types of serious cases in Texas. It's free to use and can save a lot of time when you're already dealing with so much.
